CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Set For Trial On Jan-11-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Continued To Feb-19-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Nathan Low, Esq. of FISHER & PHILLIPS LLP, T:415.490.9000, for defendant/moving party;
Un Kei Wu, Esq. of LIBERATION LAW GROUP, T:415.695.1000, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT VANTAGE MAINTENANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Vantage Maintenance's demurrer to the complaint is overruled in its entirety. Liberally construing the complaint, Plaintiffs adequately plead sufficient facts to establish an employer-employee relationship between Plaintiffs and Vantage Maintenance. (See Code Civ. Proc., 452; Compl., 2, 5, 9, 11.) Vantage Maintenance argues the allegations use boilerplate language, but at the outset of litigation, the amount of specificity in Plaintiffs' complaint adequately gives the Defendants notice of the issues. (See, e.g., Barsegian v. Kessler & Kessler (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 446, 451.) Similarly, at the pleading stage, Plaintiffs do not have to specifically name which of the alleged joint employers were liable for the acts upon which liability is based. (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 872.) The issue of whether Vantage Maintenance employed Plaintiffs is a factual issue not suitable for determination on demurrer. (See, e.g., Richtek USA, Inc. uPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 660 ["A demurrer is simply not the appropriate procedure for determining the truth of disputed facts."].)

Likewise, the complaint is neither uncertain nor ambiguous. A "demurrer for uncertainty is strictly construed, even where a complaint is in some respects uncertain, because ambiguities can be clarified under modern discovery procedures." (Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616; see also Lickiss v. Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1125, 1135 ["demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored, and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond."].) Further, whether or not Vantage Maintenance employed Plaintiffs is presumptively within Vantage's knowledge. (Chen v. Berenjian (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 811, 822 [A demurrer for uncertainty will almost certainly be overruled where the facts alleged in the complaint are presumptively within the knowledge of the demurring party or ascertainable by invoking discovery procedures.])

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Christine Jordan CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Continued To Jan-06-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Un Kei Wu, Esq. of LIBERATION LAW GROUP, T:415.695.1000, for plaintiff/opposing party;
No appearance by defendant.

RULING: Prior to the hearing, defendant's counsel notified the court and plaintiff's counsel that he (defendant's counsel) could not appear at the hearing because of a family emergency. Therefore, there being no objection from plaintiff's counsel, and for good cause, the Court sets aside the tentative ruling and

DEFENDANT VANTAGE MAINTENANCE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to January 6, 2020 at 9:30 am in Department 302.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
